sections.title: Context
sections.content:
| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| Market | Facilities Support Services |
| NAICS | 561210 |
| Product Job | Help Door & Exit Monitoring System detect unauthorized emergency exit door activations and produce audit-ready event logs for FM compliance workflows — without wired infrastructure or on-site inspection rounds |
| Technology class | Emergency Exit Security Devices with Remote Monitoring |
| Commodity FP | Prevent unauthorized use of emergency exit doors while maintaining unimpeded escape capability and alerting to door activation via audible alarm |
| Constraints in play | C3 (range), C4 (battery life), C6 (protocol lock-in), C7 (price premium), C8 (GDPR) |

Top underserved needs (score ≥ 14): N04 (16), N08 (16), N14 (16), N02 (15), N13 (15), N03 (14), N05 (14), N17 (14)

---

sections.title: Step 09 — Technology-Level Feature-Market Fit
sections.content:
### 09a — Kano Classification + ODI Scoring

| # | Feature | Kano | Time | Cost | Safety | Reliability | Skill | Stress | Pain | Confidence | Delight | **Overall** |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| F1 | Mechanical Alarm Trigger | must_be | 4 | 4 | 9 | 8 | 5 | 6 | 4 | 8 | 2 | **5.56** |
| F2 | Key Override | must_be | 5 | 3 | 5 | 4 | 6 | 6 | 5 | 5 | 2 | **4.56** |
| F3 | IoT Connectivity | one_dimensional | 8 | 6 | 7 | 7 | 5 | 7 | 8 | 7 | 6 | **6.78** |
| F4 | Remote Monitoring Dashboard | attractive | 8 | 7 | 6 | 6 | 6 | 8 | 9 | 8 | 8 | **7.33** |
| F5 | Predictive Maintenance | attractive | 7 | 7 | 6 | 8 | 5 | 7 | 7 | 7 | 6 | **6.67** |
| F6 | Regulatory Compliance Documentation | attractive | 9 | 8 | 7 | 7 | 7 | 8 | 9 | 9 | 7 | **7.89** |
| F7 | Retrofit Capability | one_dimensional | 8 | 9 | 4 | 5 | 8 | 6 | 9 | 6 | 7 | **6.89** |

`overall_fit = (Time + Cost + Safety + Reliability + Skill + Stress + Pain + Confidence + Delight) / 9`

### 09b — Rationale per Feature

| Feature | Kano rationale | Primary ODI drivers | Market evidence |
|---|---|---|---|
| **F1 Mechanical Alarm Trigger** | `must_be`: Every exit monitoring solution must produce an audible alarm; mechanical power-independence is expected once fire codes are known. Absence causes disqualification; presence is taken for granted. | Safety (9) — fail-safe under zero-voltage directly addresses N07; Confidence (8) — FM tech trusts mechanical trigger won't fail under battery depletion. | N07: "Minimize alarm failure rate under power-loss test" (Imp=10, Sat=7). |
| **F2 Key Override** | `must_be`: FM field technicians must access doors routinely without triggering monitored alarms. All FM-grade alternatives offer this. Absence would block FM deployment entirely. | Skill (6) + Stress (6): reduces protocol overhead during routine maintenance visits. | P1 Maintenance scenario: FM tech conducts battery replacement — key override prevents false-alarm event from polluting compliance logs. |
| **F3 IoT Connectivity** | `one_dimensional`: More reliable and lower-latency wireless reporting scales proportionally to FM value. FM operators choosing between mechanical-only (Alt C, 20% share, declining) and wireless specifically because of this capability. | Pain (8) — eliminates conduit wiring runs (N02, score 15); Time (8) — real-time alerts vs. manual rounds (N06); Stress (7) — FM tech confident every event is captured (N22). | N02 (score=15 UNDERSERVED): wired installation labor is the dominant cost friction in FM retrofit. N08 (score=16 UNDERSERVED): undetected activations. |
| **F4 Remote Monitoring Dashboard** | `attractive`: FM operators currently use manual inspection rounds or per-building wired panels. Portfolio-level real-time visibility is not available with any mechanical or single-site alternative. Absence does not block core alarm function but blocks the FM service differentiation model. | Pain (9) — directly eliminates manual compliance certificate compilation (N13, score=15); Delight (8) — FM manager presents live dashboard to client; Stress (8) — no undiscovered coverage gaps (N22). | N13 (score=15 UNDERSERVED): "labor-hours to compile audit-ready certificates." N15/N21: portfolio dashboard is FM's client-facing value-add. |
| **F5 Predictive Maintenance** | `attractive`: Battery-powered wireless devices create a unique maintenance burden (Alt B, P1 Maintenance) not shared by hardwired alternatives. Proactive battery alerts transform this from a reactive emergency callout risk into a scheduled CMMS work order. | Reliability (8) — N03 directly: battery depletion events that reach critical failure before CMMS alert (score=14 UNDERSERVED); Cost (7) — eliminates emergency dispatch cost premium. | N03 (score=14 UNDERSERVED): "minimize battery depletion events reaching critical failure without CMMS alert." N10 targets critical depletion ratio → 0. |
| **F6 Regulatory Compliance Documentation** | `attractive`: Automated timestamped event logs are the highest-value FM differentiator. Manual log assembly is the dominant compliance labor cost. Automated documentation directly addresses two of the three top-scoring underserved needs. | Time (9) + Pain (9) — N13: eliminates manual compilation hours; Confidence (9) — N14: tamper-evident logs as primary audit evidence; Stress (8) — FM engineer never surprised at unannounced inspection (N22). | N14 (score=16 UNDERSERVED): "minimize event log gaps that cause audit failure." N13 (score=15): "minimize FM labor hours to compile inspection certificates." N20: tamper-evident log = P3 Role identity for FM compliance engineer. |
| **F7 Retrofit Capability** | `one_dimensional`: FM contracts are predominantly retrofit (existing buildings). More retrofit-friendly = proportionally lower installation cost per door. Direct competitor axis vs. hardwired Alt A (45% share) where cable cost + electrician sign-off determines project economics. | Cost (9) — N02: eliminates conduit cost entirely; Pain (9) — removes the single most painful step in FM emergency exit deployments; Time (8) + Skill (8) — N01: 0.5-1 hr/door vs. 2-4 hr for hardwired; no electrician required. | N02 (score=15 UNDERSERVED): hardwired cable install cost is the primary barrier to switching from Alt E (do nothing) or upgrading from Alt C to IoT monitoring. N01: installation labor per door directly determines FM margin. |

### 09c — Feature-Need Coverage Map

| Need | Score | F1 | F2 | F3 | F4 | F5 | F6 | F7 |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| N04 — commissioning devices that fail under real conditions | 16 | ● | | ◐ | | | | |
| N08 — undetected door openings | 16 | | | ● | ◐ | | | |
| N14 — audit-failing log gaps | 16 | | | ◐ | ◐ | | ● | |
| N02 — hardwired cable cost in retrofit | 15 | | | ◐ | | | | ● |
| N13 — manual compliance certificate compilation | 15 | | | | ◐ | | ● | |
| N03 — battery depletion without CMMS alert | 14 | | | ◐ | | ● | | |
| N05 — non-compliant escape route configuration | 14 | ● | | | | | | |
| N17 — total cost-per-door at portfolio scale | 14 | | | ◐ | ◐ | ◐ | ◐ | ● |

● = primary contributor | ◐ = partial contributor

---

sections.title: Step 10 — Product-Related Needs (Burleson Product Jobs)
sections.content:
Needs that arise FROM this mechanism — would disappear if a LoRaWAN native device, BACnet-integrated wired system, or IP69K-rated device were chosen.

| ID | Statement | Source Constraint | Affected Markets | Importance | Satisfaction |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| PR1 | Minimize the likelihood of wireless monitoring coverage gaps in large-footprint FM client buildings caused by 868 MHz EasyWave signal attenuation through reinforced concrete and steel structures — requiring repeater chain planning that adds per-door infrastructure cost | Wireless Range Limitation (C3) | Facilities Support Services, General Warehousing, Airports | 7.0 | 3.0 |
| PR2 | Minimize the likelihood of EasyWave radio module battery depletion below monitoring threshold between scheduled FM maintenance visits in high-traffic emergency exit deployments where door activation frequency exceeds 5 events per day | IoT Battery Life Floor (C4) | Facilities Support Services, Hospitals, Schools | 8.0 | 4.0 |
| PR3 | Minimize the integration rework required to relay EasyWave-protocol alarm events to client BMS systems standardized on BACnet or KNX — caused by the absence of native open-protocol output on the wireless transmitter requiring a GfS-proprietary BUS gateway intermediary | EasyWave Protocol Lock-In (C6) | Facilities Support Services, Hotels, Hospitals, Smart Office | 7.5 | 3.5 |
| PR4 | Minimize the likelihood of FM contract specification rejection in premium-specification client buildings due to surface-mounted door guardian housing protrusion being unacceptable under interior design or heritage conservation rules | Retrofit-Only Surface Mount (C5) | Facilities Support Services, Hotels, Museums | 5.0 | 5.0 |

**Litmus test applied:**
- PR1: A LoRaWAN or wired system has different range physics → disappears ✓
- PR2: A wired or larger-battery device has no 9V depletion problem → disappears ✓
- PR3: A BACnet-native or KNX-native device has no gateway intermediary step → disappears ✓
- PR4: A flush-integrated or wired device has no surface protrusion → disappears ✓

---

sections.title: Strategic Summary
sections.content:
| Layer | Finding |
|---|---|
| Highest-fit features | F6 (7.89) and F4 (7.33) — both `attractive`; together address all three top-scoring underserved needs (N04/N08/N14) |
| Primary displacement lever | F7 (6.89, `one_dimensional`) + F3 (6.78) — the retrofit+wireless combination is the direct cost argument against hardwired Alt A (45% share) |
| Must-be baseline | F1 + F2 — table stakes; low overall fit because FM market takes them for granted; absence causes disqualification |
| Critical product-related risk | PR2 (battery at high-traffic doors, Imp=8.0, Sat=4.0) — the Türwächter's own maintenance burden (Alt B P1 Maintenance) is the single biggest barrier in high-frequency FM deployments |
| Key development gap | N04 (score=16): commissioning-passing devices that fail under real conditions — not fully addressed by any feature; requires commissioning validation tooling beyond device hardware |

---

entities.statement: Minimize the likelihood of wireless monitoring coverage gaps in large-footprint FM client buildings caused by 868 MHz EasyWave signal attenuation through reinforced concrete and steel structures — requiring repeater chain planning that adds per-door infrastructure cost

Minimize the likelihood of EasyWave radio module battery depletion below monitoring threshold between scheduled FM maintenance visits in high-traffic emergency exit deployments where door activation frequency exceeds 5 events per day

Minimize the integration rework required to relay EasyWave-protocol alarm events to client BMS systems standardized on BACnet or KNX — caused by the absence of native open-protocol output on the wireless transmitter requiring a GfS-proprietary BUS gateway intermediary

Minimize the likelihood of FM contract specification rejection in premium-specification client buildings due to surface-mounted door guardian housing protrusion being unacceptable under interior design or heritage conservation requirements
affected_markets: Museums, Historical Sites, and Similar Institutions
